name: Materials
summary: A collection of Materials objects. Each model contains a Materials collection that can be accessed via Model.materials.

methods.summary: The #[] method is used to retrieve a material by index or name.
methods.remarks: The #[] method is used to retrieve a material by index or name. The #at method is an alias of #[]

params.name: index
params.doc: A number representing the material's index in an array of Material objects.

returns.doc: a Material object on success, Nil on failure

methods.name: add
methods.remarks: Add a new Material. When called with no arguments, this will generate a new unique name for the new Material. If a name is given, it will check to see if there is already a material with that name. If there is already a material with the given name, then a new unique name is generated using the given name as a base.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of the new material.

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the materials collection.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the materials collection.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.summary: The #[] method is used to retrieve a material by index or name.
methods.remarks: The #[] method is used to retrieve a material by index or name. The #at method is an alias of #[]

params.name: index
params.doc: A number representing the material's index in an array of Material objects.

returns.doc: a Material object on success, Nil on failure

methods.name: count
methods.summary: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module. Prior to that the #count method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each.
methods.remarks: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each. This would change the size of the collection and cause elements to be skipped as the indices change. Instead copy the current collection to an array using to_a and then use each on the array, when removing content. The #each method is used to iterate through all of the materials.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: Note: The returned number includes Image materials as well.
methods.remarks: Note: The returned number includes Image materials as well. It will not reflect the number of materials yielded by #each. To get the number of non-image materials use #count or materials.to_a.size. The number of materials in the collection.

methods.name: load
methods.summary: The #load method is used to load a material from file into the model.
methods.remarks: The #load method is used to load a material from file into the model. If a matching material exist in the model it will be returned instead.

params.name: filename
params.doc: the path to the SKM file to load.

returns.doc: the new loaded material, or existing material.

throws.when: if the material failed to load.

methods.name: purge_unused
methods.summary: The purge_unused method is used to remove unused materials.
methods.remarks: The purge_unused method is used to remove unused materials.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the materials collection.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the materials collection.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: Note: The returned number includes Image materials as well.
methods.remarks: Note: The returned number includes Image materials as well. It will not reflect the number of materials yielded by #each. To get the number of non-image materials use #count or materials.to_a.size. The number of materials in the collection. The #size method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: unique_name
methods.summary: The #unique_name method is used to retrieve a unique name from the materials collection that is based on the provided one.
methods.remarks: The #unique_name method is used to retrieve a unique name from the materials collection that is based on the provided one. If provided name is unique it will be returned, otherwise any trailing indices will be replaced by a new index.

properties.name: current
properties.summary: The current method is used to get the current material, i.
properties.remarks: The current method is used to get the current material, i.e. the material that the user has selected in the Materials dialog.
